title: Flavorful Tunisian Couscous Tagine
description: A tangy, aerated tagine with chicken, preserved lemon, olives, and apricot jam served over fluffy couscous, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Heat the kazan cauldron over medium heat and add olive oil, letting it shimmer.
Add the whole chicken thighs, browning them on all sides until golden, then transfer to a bowl.
In the same cauldron, sauté diced onion and minced garlic until fragrant and translucent.
Toss in sliced carrots, preserved lemon, and green olives, cooking for 5 minutes.
Stir in the apricot jam, dried oregano, ground cumin, and salt, letting flavors meld for 2 minutes.
Return the browned chicken to the cauldron, cover, and pour in enough water to barely submerge the meat, then bring to a simmer.
After 25 minutes, remove the lid, add couscous, stir, and replace the lid.
Steam the couscous on low heat for 10 minutes, then remove from heat and fluff with a fork.
Reduce the sauce by half over low heat, creating a slightly thickened, emulsified coating.
Plate the couscous, top with chicken pieces, spoon sauce over, and garnish with a few olives.
Allow the tagine to cool to room temperature before portioning into meal‑prep containers.
Refrigerate the containers; the dish tastes best after 24 hours and can be reheated in microwave.
